title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Execution Trace: Pathvalue Escaped Segments
summary: A Go 1.26 service mixes legacy prefix routes with method and wildcard patterns, conflicts at startup, misroutes escaped slashes, and assumes slash redirects still use a permanent status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo pathvalue escaped segments increment in validated ServeMux route and precedence table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.
Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
